steps.title: Understand what the blank must mean
steps.content: The passage says Gilliam “explored a different style” and the blank fills in what he was doing with “quilt-like paintings.” The blank needs a verb that describes an artist actively producing or making new work in that style.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘predicting’: Predicting means declaring something in advance; Gilliam was actually making the paintings, not forecasting them. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘refusing’: Refusing means rejecting, which contradicts the passage—he explored this style, he did not reject it. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘hiding’: Hiding means concealing from view, but nothing in the text suggests Gilliam tried to conceal his paintings. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘creating’: Creating means producing or bringing into existence, which is exactly what an artist does when exploring a new style. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
